'''Matt Gafa''' (born 31 August 1978) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He was a [[Malta national rugby league team|Malta]] international. He last played for [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]] in the [[Super League]]. He had previously played for the [[Canberra Raiders]]<ref>[https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/london/4588116.stm BBC SPORT | Rugby League | Super League | Harlequins | Quins swoop for Australian Gafa]. BBC News (6 January 2006). Retrieved on 2017-08-24.</ref> in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] and has also played in [[Elite One Championship]] in France.<ref name="McDermott in Paul Plea">{{cite web|url=http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/08/02/18/RUGBYL_Super_McDermott.html|title=McDermott in Paul Plea|publisher=Sportinglife|date=9 February 2008|accessdate=20 February 2008}}</ref>
Gafa's usual position was as a {{rlp|ce}}. He could also operate on the {{rlp|wg}}. In 2007, he has moved into the forwards in the {{rlp|sr}}, and has also operated at {{rlp|lf}}. In the past he had also operated in the {{rlp|so}} role.

==Playing career==
Gafa made his first grade debut for Canberra in Round 1 1997 against Cronulla-Sutherland.  In 2003, Gafa was a member of the reserve grade premiership winning team scoring an individual 348 points.  

After a six-year absence from first grade, Gafa returned to the team in 2004 and became a regular starter before departing for England at the end of 2005.  Gafa joined Harlequins in 2006 who were previously known as the [[London Broncos]].

At one point in his career at Harlequins, he was the leading try-scorer in 2008's [[Super League XIII]] running in 4-tries in the opening two rounds of the engage [[Super League]].<ref name="Gafa Grabs Top Spot">{{cite web|url=http://www.quins.co.uk/newspage.ink?nid=38535&newstype=p&matchid=&storytype=RL&Refid=|title=Gafa Grabs Top Spot|publisher=Quins RL|date=19 February 2008|accessdate=19 February 2008}}</ref>

Gafa left Harlequins at the end of 2009 to return to Australia.  Gafa then became captain of the Belconnen United Scholars in the Canberra Raiders Cup.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.raiders.com.au/news/2018/05/31/forever-green-where-are-they-now---matt-gafa/|title=Forever Green: Where are they now? - Matt Gafa|website=Canberra Raiders}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/gafas-finally-made-the-cut-with-raiders-20040801-gdjgnk.html/|title=Gafa's finally made the cut with Raiders|website=www.smh.com.au}}</ref>

==Post playing==
Gafa currently works for the Australian Bureau of Statistics in Belconnen.

Gafa is currently coaching North Canberra Bears in the George Tooke Shield
